texto: ...Her boyfriend gives her a Mercedes, [her friends] say, 'Oh, that's nice.' But her boyfriend gives her a diamond, they say, 'Oh, he's serious.' It's not just the gift of love-it's the gift of commitment. She's not jumping up and down because she got a diamond ring but because she got a guy! There are those who say you don't need diamonds. I say they're right. Just like you don't need sex.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Tom Zoellner: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “...Her boyfriend gives her a Mercedes, [her friends] say, 'Oh, that's nice.' But her boyfriend gi…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amizade, advertising, diamond pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e diamond.

It's not just the gift of love-it's the gift of commitment. She's not jumping up and down because she got a diamond ring but because she got a guy! There are those who say you don't need diamonds.
